feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue in SAP Build Process Automation action sending empty list payload to SAP CAP action.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are building a SAP CAP application where we define an action to store list data into a HANA DB after an approval step in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

Here is the setup.
My CAP application schema looks like this.
entity OTPDA : managed {
key userID : String;
issueDate : String;
payComponent : String;
payComponentNature : String;
amount : String;
currency : String;
unit : String;
tmu : String;
assignmentNumber : String;
comments : String;
log : String;
}


CAP action in service section
action storeOtpdaData(OtpdaDatas : array of OTPDA) returns String;

and its handler

const onStoreOtpdaData = async (req) => {
const { OTPDA } = cds.entities("db.payroll");
const tx = cds.transaction(req);
const aOtpdaDatas = req.data.OtpdaDatas;

try {
for (const entry of aOtpdaDatas) {
await tx.run(
UPSERT.into(OTPDA).entries({
userID: entry.userID,
issueDate: entry.issueDate,
payComponent: entry.payComponent,
payComponentNature: entry.payComponentNature,
amount: entry.amount,
currency: entry.currency,
unit: entry.unit,
tmu: entry.tmu,
assignmentNumber: entry.assignmentNumber,
comments: entry.comments,
log: entry.log
})
);
}
return `${aOtpdaDatas.length} record(s) stored successfully.`;
} catch (error) {
console.error("Error inserting OTPDA data:", error);
req.reject(500, "Failed to store OTPDA records.");
}
};

In SAP Build Process Automation, I have created an action using the CAP destination and mapped it accordingly. However, when BPA tries to call the CAP action, the request payload is going blank, and therefore no records are being stored.

Here is the screenshot:- The request section payload structure is array of object  but the request body which BPA is sending is array of blank objects. :-


Any insights or guidance would be highly appreciated.
Thanks a lot in advance!

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Cloud Application Programming Model SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation (trial) instance could not be created
entry.content:
hello,

I want to create a SAP Build Process Automation instance in my trial account.

When trying to create it fails with "Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community."

I've tried it several times in the last days, always same result.

Is there any solution to get SAP Build Process Automation running again?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: BTP Build Process Automation Job Status Remains As Ready
entry.content:
Hello,

We are experiencing a problem in our BTP Build Process Automation system. The Job we created remains in the "Ready" state and gives an error because it is not progressing. The job cannot be run. However, when the same automation is tested manually, it works without any problems. The automation is deployed in "Public" and scheduled as "Scheduled" type. Destination connection and Environments connections have been established successfully. There is no input in the automation, data is received with the "Call Web Service with Destination" tool and Automation is selected as the Scheduler operator.

Detailed information is provided as follows. I kindly request your support for the solution of the problem.

We want to set up a job for the automation we created on SAP BTP Build Process Automation. The job we created remains in "Ready" status, and because the status does not progress, the job gives an error after 1 day and does not work. However, when the automation is run manually for testing, it works without any issues.

1- Error displayed on screen
When we checked for the "Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution reached" error shown in the job, we found that there is only one Agent.

2- Job Setup Process
The created automation has been deployed in "Public".

A scheduler has been set up with the Scheduled type for the deployed project.

The Destination connection has been successfully established. The connection for Environments has been set up.

3- Scheduler Details
We do not have any inputs for the Automation. Data is obtained using the "Call Web Service with Destination" tool.

For the scheduler runner, Automation has been selected.

4- Automation Details
In the first 13 steps of our application, there are processes for retrieving and filtering appropriate data. In the subsequent processes, appropriate operations are performed on the screens we recorded. When the Automation is run manually, it works without any issues.

Step 1

Step 2


Step 3

entry.title: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post
entry.content:
Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post

Please help me to resolve this issue

entry.title: SAP Build Process – Stuck at invoiceRead Step in Invoice Processing Mission
entry.content:
Hi all, I’m following the "Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation" mission from SAP Discovery Center.

Link: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/145344/cardName/Invoice%20automatically%20processed%20and%20posted%20to%20SAP%20S%2F4HANA%20Cloud/?tab=projectboard

My process is getting stuck at the invoiceRead step—it starts but never completes, so it doesn't proceed to the condition branch. When I test the invoiceRead automation in editable mode, it works fine. However, when I run the process in deployed mode, it gets stuck at this step.

I've attached logs and screenshots showing the issue. Any ideas on how to resolve this?


I need the invoiceRead step is to be completed like the below step. Please help me in this case.

entry.title: Build Process Automation - mail attachment size
entry.content:
Is there a way to get information about mail attachment size? Currently my workaround is to save attachments and then use file system activities to retrieve the size. Is there any direct way to get this information from outlook without the need of saving the files (and deleting them later if needed)? Probably using some custom script?

Thank you.

entry.title: “quota exceeded" error in SAP Build basic trial environment
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am currently preparing for the C_LCNC_2406 (SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build) certification, which requires completion of the "Experiencing End-to-End SAP Build" system exercise ( https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/experiencing-end-to-end-sap-build ).
As part of this exercise, I am instructed to create a new process in SAP Build Process Automation using the provided SAP Build basic trial environment. Unfortunately, when attempting to create a new Process there, I encounter the following error:

Error

Permitted quota of 750 was exceeded (type: "project").

Correlation Id: e6afe57f-1cc0-47b7-64c8-762ce904183e

Has anyone experienced this issue before and knows how I can approach or resolve it?

I’ve already created a case in the SAP Learning help center, but the SAP Learning Support Team informed me that this issue lies outside their support scope as the trial environment is publicly hosted.

I truly appreciate any help or suggestions you can provide.

Best regards,
Anna

entry.title: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event based processes
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm currently following a codejam blog regarding event-based processes for sap build. here is the link of my current lesson: 3 - Explore SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh | SAP Tutorials

one of the prerequisite is a "participants handbook" which contains the system and credentials needed to follow the blog.

where can i get this? thanks

entry.title: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content:
Hi guys,
I have one demo process in my SAP Build Process Automation Application and I have deployed and published it.


Now, in my SAP Build Apps, I have created an application and in Integration tab, I selected SAP Build Library, I can see my process there, but when I click on it shows Page not found.


I have also configured sap_process_automation_service_user_access destination in my BTP for the instance of Build Process automation and test shows successful & 401. Also, added that destination in control tower's destination in build lobby. Please help.

In the attached image we can see the published process available in cloud library.

SAP Build Library

Page Not Found Error for process

Thanks & Regards,
Marsh

entry.title: How to create Parallel branches in SAP BTP Process Visibility
entry.content: I need to create parallel branches in SAP BTP Process visibility without creating process orchestration using build process automation as complete process will be running in other application we just need to monitor the process using process visibility.

entry.title: Problem with Action Project Design | Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Builders,

I have discovered a problem with the Action Project Design in the SAP Build Lobby.

I have been trying to structure a request body using the "Build from Scratch" option. However, when I save the fields, items in nested objects will change order. I deleted the fields and added them again and even made an entirely new Action Project with no success in resolving the order of the fields. I assume there is a logic problem with this tool.

In my case I need to have control over the field order or the request will cause a processing failure.

Any help in getting a this resolved would be greatly appreciated.

#SAPBuild

@SAPSupport @Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: I am currently working on a project involving SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: I need to integrate it into an existing BAPI in SAP S/4HANA. However, I'm facing an issue: I don't have access to the BAPI through the Actions group in SAP Build Process Automation. Therefore, I'm looking for a solution to connect to this BAPI via SAP BTP.

entry.title: Build Process Automation: Call Web Service with Destination Timeout Error
entry.content:
Hi community,

I am configuring a standard process template "Supplier Invoice Status Check".

https://api.sap.com/package/eu10.storecontentdevacc.automaticsupplierinvoicestatuscheck2/overview

By following the configuration guide, I have created a communication user, communication arrangement, etc. in our S/4HANA Public Cloud tenant. The Destination has been configured as shown below:

I double-checked that this destination is accessible via SAP Business Application Studio.

However, when I test the process, it fails at the first "Call Web Service with Destination" step with the following error:

		{
			"code": "irpa_core.error.Timeout",
			"message": "Timeout error: call callHttpRequest"
		}

The following is the request object passed to the step:

		{
			"url": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartnerAddress?$filter=BusinessPartner eq '15300002'&$format=json&$select=AddressID",
			"method": "GET",
			"responseType": "json",
			"resolveBodyOnly": true,
			"timeout": 30000,
			"encodeDestinationPath": true
		}

The URL is working fine from Postman (with the same user and password used in the destination).

Does anyone have an idea why this timeout error is occurring?

entry.title: Click and Mouse Click activities misbehaving only when used in For Each Loop
entry.content:
Hi Team,

My automation that clicks on a list of hyperlinks is suddenly not working as it should. The Click activity used to work no problem for every item in a For Each loop for this list of links. Now, it is clicking on the first link, but after that - nothing happens. The debugger timeline says that the second Click activity did take place but there is nothing to show for it.

So, I replaced the Click with the Mouse Click activity, which works as intended...except when I try to run the automation on a browser that is being displayed anywhere other than my laptop screen. If it is running in a browser that is displayed on - for instance, a monitor connected to the laptop via USB-C, the mouse click does click...but it always completely misses its mark! It does this on my laptop screen as well, but very rarely. What puzzles me the most is the activities seem to work in other parts of the automation fine, the only thing different about these particular activities is that they are operating on a collection in a For Each loop.

I have tried:

-Recapturing the affected screen

-Running the automation across three different browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox)

-Restarting the Desktop Agent

-Restarting the IRPA Chrome (and Edge) extension

I could try maybe reimplementing the For Each loop if this is the common denominator, but I'm not sure why or how that would help.

The irpa_core version I am on is 1.73.13.

Any sort of explanation here would help me immensely.

Thanks in advance, 

Hamzah

entry.title: Issue with IRPA Bot Handling File Upload Dialog in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm facing an issue while using the IRPA bot to automate the upload of a file in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud application.

When the bot clicks the "Upload" button, the system automatically opens the OS file manager window. I have created a screenshot (screen capture) of the file dialog and configured the necessary elements to select the appropriate file. However, when I run the bot, it does not perform any actions within the file manager.

(I'm using a trial account)
SAP Build Process Automation 
#SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation

SAP Intelligent RPA

Process Automation

Here are the steps I've followed:

1. I access the application and click the "Upload" button, which automatically opens the file manager.

2. I add a screenshot of the opened file dialog in my current application.

3. I configure the recognition criteria and declare the element I want the bot to interact with.

4. I define a screen activity in my current application to write the file name into the selected element.

5. Finally, I run the process. The bot opens the application and executes the defined activities, but when the file manager appears, it does nothing. In SAP Build, I receive the following message:

 

"Timeout error: Waiting for element Edit (manageIncomingPaymentFiles.manageIncomingPaymentFiles.edit) to appear (30 s)."
 

Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Build App event handler issue
entry.content:
Hey guys

I have configured sap_process_automation_service_user_access destination in my BTP for the instance of Build Process automation and test shows successful & 401. Also, added that destination in control tower's destination in build lobby

I have also created a Web & Mobile application in Sap Build App 

And I have integrated a process (Sap Build Automation) into Sap Build App. 

When I preview my application, I'm encountering an error in SAP Build App: Unknown node id: page:1:component:<ID>:onFocus/onBlur/onKeyPress/onChange when interacting with input fields. The error mentions Abort fetching component for route: "/[appId]/[appName]/[...params]". I don't have any logic configured for these events, can anyone help me identify the cause of this issue?

Thank in advance, 

Giang

entry.title: Desktop Agent Not Receiving Project in Deployed Mode
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m following the "Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation" mission from SAP Discovery Center.

Link: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/145344/cardName/Invoice%20automatically%20processed%20and%20posted%20to%20SAP%20S%2F4HANA%20Cloud/?tab=projectboard

I am currently facing an issue with the SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent. The agent is running fine in editable/test mode and the automation works as expected.

However, once I deploy the project, and trigger the process via form link, the Desktop Agent does not receive the project.

The following actions have already been checked/completed:

* Agent is properly configured and in attended mode.

* Required attributes have been added to the agent and to the project.

* Connection between Desktop Agent & Tenant : Active

* The agent is assigned to the correct environment.

* Tried restarting the agent and redeploying the project.

Despite all these steps, the Desktop Agent still displays the message: "It looks like no project has been received yet. When there are, you'll see them here."

In SAP Community tab, I've seen a post related to my issue and they said "You need to create an Automation Launcher in able for your automation to be called on the machine's desktop agent.

Your project needs to be added in "Automation Launchers" to be distributed to Attended Agents:"

In my project, already automation launcher is there. But I can't able to add my project under "Automation Launchers" tab.

Even, i have pay-as-you-go subscription plan for sap build process automation, with attended-automation plan subscribed, assigned quota but i get the same error, when i try to run automation launcher , SAP_IPA_05066: Execution is not permitted on tenant "xxxxxxx" .

Can someone please help identify what might be missing or misconfigured? Any guidance or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Data getting erased in quickmodel of signavio(using process automation)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,I'm working on automating SAP Signavio QuickModel using SAP Process Automation. I'm capturing the page and defining elements like Organization, Start, End, What, and the "+" button using the Application functionality.

Issue: As soon as I click the "+" button, the value previously entered in the "What" field disappears. I have tried:

Using SendKeys (Tab) after entering the "What" value,

Adding Wait and Wait (element) before and after each step,

Switching recognition criteria to class and id only,

Using SetFocus before entering the value,

And even entering the value after clicking the "+" button — but the value still gets cleared or doesn’t persist.

This behavior is consistent regardless of wait time or method used. The "+" click seems to re-render or refresh the input fields, which causes the data loss.

while Doing it manual it works fine ,but it finds this issue by doing it from process automation

Has anyone faced a similar issue or found a workaround? Is there a way to reliably retain the "What" input or programmatically set it after DOM refresh?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: How to bring the Approve/Reject step from SAP My Inbox into my custom SAP Build App
entry.content:
Hey guys, 

I'm building an application allows admin can approve/reject those order when user create their order in sap build process automation

So the question i want to know the answer is : How to bring the Approve/Reject step from SAP My Inbox into my custom SAP Build Apps application

Thank in advance, 

Giang

entry.title: OpenID redirect error when accessing SAP Build Process Automation API from Microsoft Power Apps.
entry.content:
I can instantiate a BPA instance fine using POSTMAN but am having issues doing the same in Microsoft Power Apps (MPA) custom connection.

The MPA connection is setup to call https://ndbs-build.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize  using the client credentials but is returning the following BTP error..

OpenID provider cannot process the request because the redirect_uri does not match the configuration. Please contact your system administrator.

I believe the reason it works in POSTMAN is because there is no redirect url, but in MPA this is enforced as 

https://global.consent.azure-apim.net/redirect/(id).

I've looked into possible resolutions and it looks like I need to include the redirect in BTP like so...

 

"oauth2-configuration": {

"redirect-uris": [

"<MPA Url>"

]

}

However, this is a BPA instance that has it's own uaa, which cannot be edited, so I'm not sure how the redirect-uris can be added.

Another suggestion is to add the redirect uri in Security > Oauth clients, which is not showing. 

Assuming I can get Admin access, what is the correct approach to allow a BPA instance to be called from MPA?
